item.title: Advanced therapies in management of pediatric inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Current opinion in pediatrics - Pediatric inflammatory bowel disease (PIBD) has a rising global prevalence, with patients often demonstrating more aggressive and extensive disease activity compared to adult-onset disease. With im...

item.title: Age-Related Microbial Differences in Newly Diagnosed Crohn's Disease Reveal the Distinct Enrichment of Oral-Associated Taxa in Pediatric Patients.
item.description: Gut and liver - Crohn's disease (CD) presents differently by age of onset, but the microbial distinctions between pediatric and adult CD are currently unclear. In this study, the gut microbiota of fecal samples co...

item.title: Transition practices of patients with inflammatory bowel disease from pediatric to adult healthcare systems. Results from a national survey.
item.description: Archives de pediatrie : organe officiel de la Societe francaise de pediatrie - Although recommendations for structured transition programs in adolescents with Inflammatory Bowel Diseases (IBD) have been published, few studies have addressed the implementation of transition in...

item.title: Escalating anti-tumour necrosis factor exposure with reductions in surgical resection rates in paediatric inflammatory bowel disease: an 18-year real-world population-based cohort study.
item.description: BMJ open gastroenterology - To evaluate the relationship between anti-tumour necrosis factor (TNF) use and surgical resection rates in a regional paediatric inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) cohort. This retrospective cohort s...

item.title: Benefit-risk balance of advanced therapies in pediatric inflammatory bowel disease: evidence and gaps versus adults.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Although the incidence of pediatric Inflammatory Bowel Diseases (IBD) continues to rise globally, often presenting with more aggressive phenotypes than in adults, the therapeutic arsenal available ...

item.title: Biologic drugs for induction and maintenance of remission in Crohn's disease: a network meta-analysis.
item.description: The Cochrane database of systematic reviews - Systematic review - Crohn's disease is an inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) that can extend to any part of the gut. Many people with Crohn's disease fail to reach and maintain remission using conventional therapies. Bi...

item.title: Racial and Ethnic Differences in Ostomy Creation in Pediatric Patients With Crohn's Disease.
item.description: The Journal of surgical research - Crohn's disease (CD) has historically been more prevalent among non-Hispanic White populations; however, recent studies demonstrate rising incidence in non-White populations. Despite this shift, li...

item.title: Risk factors for cutaneous complications of advanced therapies in paediatric inflammatory bowel disease patients.
item.description: Pediatric research - Observational data suggest comparable efficacy of different advanced therapies in paediatric inflammatory bowel disease (pIBD). Cutaneous adverse effects (cAEs) of tumour necrosis factor antagonist...

item.title: Correction: Incidence of Clostridium difficile infection and enteric pathogens in children with inflammatory bowel disease presenting with disease exacerbation.
item.description: Frontiers in pediatrics - [This corrects the article DOI: 10.3389/fped.2026.1808589.].

item.title: Risk of disseminated tuberculosis and other infections after neonatal Bacillus Calmette-Guérin vaccination in infants with in-utero exposure to tumor necrosis factor-α inhibitors.
